title: Emergency Inverter Shutdown
steps: Notify control room and site supervisor immediately.
Isolate the affected inverter using the AC/DC disconnect switches.
Confirm DC string voltage has dropped to safe level (<50V) using the string combiner meter.
Log the fault code from the inverter HMI and photograph the display.
Raise a corrective maintenance work order in the CMMS.
Do NOT attempt to restart until root cause has been identified and cleared.
warnings: Do not open DC isolators under load.
Ensure PPE (arc flash rated) before approaching MV panel.

title: Grid Over-Frequency Response
steps: Acknowledge the over-frequency alarm in the SCADA system.
Confirm live frequency reading on grid monitoring display (should be >50.2 Hz).
Check if automatic curtailment has been triggered by the plant controller.
If auto-curtailment failed, manually reduce plant output by 10% per minute until frequency normalises.
Notify the TSO (Transmission System Operator) on the dedicated hotline.
Log event start time, peak frequency, and duration in the Grid Event Log.
Resume normal output once frequency is stable within 49.8–50.2 Hz for 5 consecutive minutes.
warnings: Do not exceed 10 MW/min ramp rate during recovery.
Coordinate ramp-up with TSO before resuming full output.

title: LVRT (Low Voltage Ride-Through) Event Response
steps: Confirm LVRT event on SCADA — note voltage dip depth (pu) and duration (ms).
Check whether the plant rode through or tripped offline.
If tripped: verify protection relay logs to confirm correct LVRT curve application.
If rode through: confirm reactive power injection was activated and log Q dispatch value.
Download event recorder data from the affected site within 2 hours.
Submit LVRT event report to grid compliance team within 24 hours.
For trip events: do not reconnect until TSO confirmation received.
warnings: LVRT trip without TSO authorisation is a grid code violation.
Check IEC 61400-25 / DEWA grid code LVRT curve before relay resetting.

title: BESS Thermal Runaway Prevention
steps: Acknowledge BATT-TEMP-HIGH alarm immediately.
Check BMS dashboard — identify affected module rack and cell string.
If temperature >55°C: initiate controlled DC isolation for the affected rack via BMS.
Verify HVAC cooling system is operating at maximum capacity.
Do NOT open the battery enclosure door if temperature is still rising.
Notify fire safety team and have CO2 suppression system on standby.
If temperature exceeds 65°C or smoke detected: initiate full site evacuation and call emergency services.
Log incident with exact temperature readings, SOC at event time, and rack ID.
warnings: IEC 62619 thermal runaway protocol — follow strictly.
Do not attempt to discharge the battery during a thermal event.

title: Wind Turbine Emergency Stop
steps: From SCADA, issue Emergency Stop command to the affected turbine.
Confirm turbine has feathered blades and rotor speed is <5 RPM.
Lock out / tag out (LOTO) the turbine at the main MV switch at tower base.
Do not ascend the tower until rotor is fully stopped and locked.
Check gearbox oil temperature and brake system status from the SCADA alarms.
Raise maintenance work order and notify the O&M team.
Report event to TSO if the turbine contributes >10 MW to the grid.
warnings: Never enter the nacelle while rotor is spinning.
High wind speed lock-out: if wind >25 m/s, approach to tower base only.

title: Daily Plant Start-Up
steps: Log into SCADA and confirm all communications are healthy (green on comms dashboard).
Check weather forecast — note irradiance, wind speed, and ambient temperature.
Review overnight alarm log — acknowledge and escalate any unresolved critical alarms.
Confirm grid frequency is stable (49.8–50.2 Hz) before enabling generation.
Enable plant in Auto mode via the Plant Controller.
Monitor ramp-up rate — ensure <10 MW/min per SEC/DEWA grid code requirements.
Verify reactive power set-point is correct per TSO dispatch instruction.
Record start-up time and initial generation value in the shift log.

title: Inverter DC Bus Overvoltage Response
steps: Isolate the affected inverter from both AC and DC sides before any inspection.
Check DC string voltage at the combiner box to confirm overvoltage level (expect >1000 Vdc).
Inspect combiner fuses and disconnect switches in the affected bay.
Review SCADA data for sudden irradiance spike or MPPT fault codes preceding the event.
Check IGBT desaturation fault register — do not reset without root cause confirmation.
Raise a corrective maintenance work order and document fault codes and timestamps.
Do not re-energise the inverter until root cause has been identified and cleared.
warnings: Do not open DC isolators under load — risk of DC arc flash.
Wear arc-flash rated PPE (minimum CAT 2) before approaching the MV panel.
IGBT desaturation may have occurred — hardware inspection required before restart.

title: BESS Low State of Charge Response
steps: Halt all discharge operations immediately via BMS dispatch command.
Switch BESS to grid-charging mode at maximum safe charge rate per BMS limits.
Check live grid frequency — if still deviating, engage an alternative frequency support asset.
Monitor cell temperatures during fast charging — abort charging if any cell exceeds 45°C.
Notify the grid operator of reduced BESS availability and revised frequency response capacity.
Review dispatch schedule for the previous 24h — identify cause of insufficient recharge window.
Log SOC at event time, duration, and dispatch events in the shift log.
warnings: Continued discharge below 20% SOC accelerates irreversible capacity degradation.
Verify HVAC cooling is operational before initiating fast charging.
Do not exceed manufacturer rated charge rate — check BMS limits before override.

title: Wind Turbine Blade Inspection
steps: Confirm turbine is stopped and LOTO applied at MV switch at tower base.
Verify rotor is locked using the rotor lock pin before ascending tower.
Conduct visual inspection of all three blades from nacelle hatch — note leading-edge erosion, cracks, or delamination.
Deploy rope-access team or drone for close-range inspection per IEC 61400-1 Section 7.
Photograph and document all defects with GPS location tags and severity rating (0–4 scale).
Check blade root bolts for corrosion and torque compliance.
Update inspection findings in CMMS against the turbine asset record.
If leading-edge erosion >10 mm depth detected, raise emergency WO for repair before restart.
warnings: IEC 61400-1 mandates blade inspection every 2 years maximum.
Never ascend tower if wind speed >12 m/s at nacelle level.
Rotor lock must be confirmed before any physical blade access.

title: SCADA / RTU Communication Fault Response
steps: Identify the affected RTU or communication node from the SCADA topology view.
Ping the RTU IP address from the control room workstation — confirm network reachability.
Check physical Ethernet/fibre connections at the field cabinet.
Verify DNP3 / Modbus TCP polling interval and timeout settings in the SCADA configuration.
Reboot the RTU if unresponsive for >10 minutes and no active field operations are ongoing.
If communication is not restored after reboot, raise a corrective WO for RTU hardware inspection.
Log the communication gap duration and estimated data loss in the plant data quality log.
warnings: Do not reboot an RTU while live switching operations are in progress.
Communication outage may mask active alarms — increase manual patrols during the outage.
